title: House Agriculture Committee Unveils Farm Bill Draft Featuring Bioenergy and Biofuel Provisions
content: House Agriculture Committee released a draft of the new Farm Bill that includes several provisions supporting bioenergy and biofuels, reinforcing renewable fuels as part of U.S. agricultural and rural policy. The draft strengthens federal programs that promote bioenergy production, advanced biofuels, and related infrastructure. Under the Energy Title, one key provision increases the maximum loan guarantee under the Rural Energy for America Program (REAP) to $50 million. It also directs the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) to consider the potential improvements to an applicantâ€™s financial condition when scoring REAP applications, potentially making it easier for qualifying projects to secure support. The bill reflects continued efforts to integrate renewable energy and biofuel development into broader agricultural and rural economic policy, though it must still move through the legislative process before becoming law.
author: Analysts Account
